line: def DolphinLib(lib_name: str, objects: Objects, fix_epilogue=False) -> Library:

title: Update `dtk-template`
line: This file contains file splits for a module.

title: Update `dtk-template`
line: - `vaddr:` (REL only) The fixed virtual address of the section. When set, split and symbol addresses are written as absolute addresses.

title: Update `dtk-template`
line: - `path/to/file.cpp` The name of the source file, usually relative to `src`. The file does **not** need to exist to start.

title: Update `dtk-template`
line: This corresponds to an entry in `configure.py` for specifying compiler flags and other options.

title: Update `dtk-template`
line: - `comment:` Overrides the `mw_comment_version` setting in [`config.yml`](/config/GAMEID/config.example.yml) for this file. See [Comment section](comment_section.md).

title: Update `dtk-template`
line: - `comment:0` is used to disable `.comment` section generation for a file that wasn't compiled with `mwcc`.

title: Update `dtk-template`
line: This file was assembled and only contains label symbols. Generating a `.comment` section for it will crash `mwld`.

title: Update `dtk-template`
line: - `order:` Allows influencing the resolved link order of objects. This is **not required**, as decomp-toolkit will generate the link order automatically. This can be used to fine-tune the link order for ambiguous cases.

title: Update `dtk-template`
line: This ensures that `file2.cpp` is always anchored in between 1 and 3 when resolving the final link order.

title: Update `dtk-template`
line: - `start:` The start address of the section within the file. For DOLs, this is the absolute address (e.g. `0x80001234`). For RELs, this is the section-relative address (e.g. `0x1234`).

title: Update `dtk-template`
line: - `end:` The end address of the section within the file.

title: Update `dtk-template`
line: - `align:` Specifies the alignment of the section. If not specified, the default alignment for the section is used.

title: Update `dtk-template`
line: - `rename:` Writes this section under a different name when generating the split object. Used for `.ctors$10`, etc.

title: Update `dtk-template`
line: - `skip` Skips this data when writing the object file. Used for ignoring data that's linker-generated.

title: Update `dtk-template`
line: This file contains all symbols for a module, one per line.

title: Update `dtk-template`
line: Numbers can be written as decimal or hexadecimal. Hexadecimal numbers must be prefixed with `0x`.

title: Update `dtk-template`
line: Comment lines starting with `//` or `#` are permitted, but are currently **not** preserved when updating the file.

title: Update `dtk-template`
line: - `symbol_name` - The name of the symbol. (For C++, this is the mangled name, e.g. `__dt__13mDoExt_bckAnmFv`)

title: Update `dtk-template`
line: - `section` - The section the symbol is in.

title: Update `dtk-template`
line: - `address` - The address of the symbol. For DOLs, this is the absolute address (e.g. `0x80001234`). For RELs, this is the section-relative address (e.g. `0x1234`).

title: Update `dtk-template`
line: All attributes are optional, and are separated by spaces.

title: Update `dtk-template`
line: - `data:` The data type used when writing disassembly. `byte`, `2byte`, `4byte`, `8byte`, `float`, `double`, `int`, `short`, `string`, `wstring`, `string_table`, `wstring_table`, `sjis`, or `sjis_table`.

title: Update `dtk-template`
line: - `hidden` Marked as "hidden" in the generated object. (Only used for extab)

title: Update `dtk-template`
line: - `force_active` Marked as ["exported"](comment_section.md) in the generated object, and added to `FORCEACTIVE` in the generated `ldscript.lcf`. Prevents the symbol from being deadstripped.

title: Update `dtk-template`
line: - `noreloc` Prevents the _contents_ of the symbol from being interpreted as addresses. Used for objects containing data that look like pointers, but aren't.

title: Update `dtk-template`
line: - `noexport` When `export_all` is enabled, this excludes the symbol from being exported. Used for symbols that are affected by linker `-code_merging`.

title: Update `dtk-template`
line: - `stripped` Indicates a symbol that was stripped by the linker. Used for symbols that affect the [common BSS inflation bug](common_bss.md), despite not existing in the final binary.
